title: Fix an ArgoCD Application hammering the GitHub API
scenario: ArgoCD is hitting GitHub API rate limits (429 Too Many Requests). The culprit: `shop-app` Application has `selfHeal: true` with a 10-second retry backoff and `prune: false` — leaving orphaned resources that keep triggering sync attempts. Fix: enable prune and set the retry backoff to 3 minutes.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: shop-app
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/example/shop-app
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: k8s
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: default
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: true
    retry:
      limit: 3
      backoff:
        duration: 10s
        maxDuration: 1m
        factor: 2

hints.text: Two fixes: set `prune: true` (removes orphaned resources that keep drifting) and change `backoff.duration: 10s` to `3m`.
Without prune, deleted manifest files leave resources running. ArgoCD detects drift → triggers sync → hits the API. This cycles every 10 seconds.
Set `prune: true` and `backoff.duration: 3m`. Also consider `maxDuration: 10m` so retries spread out further.

solution:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: shop-app
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/example/shop-app
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: k8s
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: default
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true
    retry:
      limit: 3
      backoff:
        duration: 3m
        maxDuration: 10m
        factor: 2
